description: AI interpreter overlay. Contains intake trees, plain-English summaries, resolution principles, framing guidance, and follow-up question sets. Not a source of policy truth — always defer to ct_facts and ct_rules for authoritative content.

framing.scope: This assistant covers Gloucester City Council Tax for the 2026/27 financial year. It cannot make binding decisions or access individual account records.
tone_principles: Lead with the most beneficial outcome for the resident first.
Present special payment arrangements primarily as the way to catch up on missed payments — not as a last resort.
Flag vulnerability or hardship signals and signpost additional support proactively.
Distinguish clearly between 'disregarded' (person not counted) and 'exempt' (no liability) — residents often confuse them.
Always make clear that a final determination rests with the council and encourage the resident to apply.
resolution_principles: Where disregards are identified, emit them as intermediate reasoning before resolving the final adult count.
Multiple adjustments may apply simultaneously — do not stop at the first match.
Premiums apply to the property, not the owner — flag this when a resident is considering purchasing an empty property.
Council Tax Support is means-tested and not a discount — use different language for it.
SMI households and care leavers should be routed to application promptly — these are often unclaimed.
out_of_scope: Individual account balances or arrears amounts
Holiday lets business rates transition (officer judgement required)
Most non-student exemption classes (officer verification required)

initial_triage.description: Questions to ask first to route the resident to the right topic

questions.text: Do you know which Council Tax band your property is in (A–H)?
Is there only one adult aged 18 or over living at the property?
Is any adult in the property a full-time student, carer, severely mentally impaired, apprentice, or student nurse?
Is the property empty, a second home, or an annexe?
Are you struggling to pay your Council Tax, or on a low income?

disregards_deep_dive.description: Questions to identify which disregards may apply

questions.text: Is the person a full-time student at a college or university (course at least 1 year, 24 weeks/year, 21 hrs/week)?
Does the person provide at least 35 hours of care per week to someone who is not their spouse, partner or child under 18?
Does the person they care for receive a qualifying disability benefit (e.g. PIP, Attendance Allowance, DLA)?
Has a doctor certified that a person in the property is severely mentally impaired, and do they receive a qualifying benefit?
Has the property been adapted for a disabled resident's needs (e.g. extra bathroom, wider doorways, essential room)?
How long has the property been empty and unfurnished?
Is the property furnished but not used as anyone's main or sole residence?
Is there an annexe attached to the main property? Is it occupied by a relative or used as part of the main home?

single-person-discount.plain_english_summary: Single Person Discount: 25% reduction. Only one person aged 18 or over lives in the property as their sole or main residence
single-person-discount.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the Single Person Discount?

severely-mentally-impaired.plain_english_summary: Severely Mentally Impaired Discount: Either 25% or 100% depending on household composition reduction. See eligibility details.
severely-mentally-impaired.effect: Either 25% or 100% depending on household composition
severely-mentally-impaired.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the Severely Mentally Impaired Discount?

care-leavers.plain_english_summary: Care Leavers Discount: 100% reduction. See eligibility details.
care-leavers.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the Care Leavers Discount?

carer-disregard.plain_english_summary: Carer Disregard: Carer is disregarded - may result in 25% discount if only one other adult reduction. See eligibility details.
carer-disregard.effect: Carer is disregarded - may result in 25% discount if only one other adult
carer-disregard.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the Carer Disregard?

full-time-students.plain_english_summary: Full-time Student Disregard: Students disregarded - 100% discount if all adults are students, otherwise may result in 25% if one student with one other adult reduction. See eligibility details.
full-time-students.effect: Students disregarded - 100% discount if all adults are students, otherwise may result in 25% if one student with one other adult
full-time-students.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the Full-time Student Disregard?

apprentices-youth-trainees.plain_english_summary: Apprentice and Youth Trainee Disregard: Disregarded - may result in discount depending on household reduction. See eligibility details.
apprentices-youth-trainees.effect: Disregarded - may result in discount depending on household
apprentices-youth-trainees.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the Apprentice and Youth Trainee Disregard?

school-college-leavers.plain_english_summary: School/College Leaver Disregard (Age 18-19): Disregarded until 1 November reduction. Left school or college after 30 April, aged 18-19
school-college-leavers.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the School/College Leaver Disregard (Age 18-19)?

disabled-band-reduction.plain_english_summary: Disabled Band Reduction: One band lower (Band A gets 1/9th reduction) reduction. See eligibility details.
disabled-band-reduction.effect: One band lower (Band A gets 1/9th reduction)
disabled-band-reduction.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the Disabled Band Reduction?

unoccupied-unfurnished.plain_english_summary: Empty Property Discount (Unoccupied and Unfurnished): 25% for up to 6 months reduction. Property is unoccupied and unfurnished
unoccupied-unfurnished.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the Empty Property Discount (Unoccupied and Unfurnished)?

major-repair.plain_english_summary: Major Repair Discount: 25% for up to 12 months reduction. Property unoccupied and undergoing major structural alteration or repair to make it habitable
major-repair.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the Major Repair Discount?

annexe-discount.plain_english_summary: Annexe Discount: 50% reduction. See eligibility details.
annexe-discount.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the Annexe Discount?

job-related-second-home.plain_english_summary: Job-Related Second Home Discount: 50% reduction. See eligibility details.
job-related-second-home.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the Job-Related Second Home Discount?

hospital-care-patients.plain_english_summary: Hospital/Care Patients: Disregarded - not counted as adults for Council Tax reduction. See eligibility details.
hospital-care-patients.effect: Disregarded - not counted as adults for Council Tax
hospital-care-patients.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the Hospital/Care Patients?

persons-in-detention.plain_english_summary: Persons in Detention: Disregarded - not counted as adults for Council Tax reduction. See eligibility details.
persons-in-detention.effect: Disregarded - not counted as adults for Council Tax
persons-in-detention.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the Persons in Detention?

members-of-religious-communities.plain_english_summary: Members of Religious Communities: Disregarded - not counted as adults for Council Tax reduction. Members of religious communities whose principal occupation is prayer, contemplation, education, or relief of suffering
members-of-religious-communities.effect: Disregarded - not counted as adults for Council Tax
members-of-religious-communities.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the Members of Religious Communities?

foreign-spouses-dependants-of-students.plain_english_summary: Foreign Spouses/Dependants of Students: Disregarded - not counted as adults for Council Tax reduction. Non-British spouses/dependants of students prevented by Home Office from working or claiming benefits
foreign-spouses-dependants-of-students.effect: Disregarded - not counted as adults for Council Tax
foreign-spouses-dependants-of-students.resident_question: Do you think you might qualify for the Foreign Spouses/Dependants of Students?

single-person-discount: Has anyone else moved in recently?
Is anyone in the property disregarded (e.g. student, carer, SMI)?
council-tax-support: Are you working, claiming benefits, or retired?
Do you own or rent your home?
empty-homes-premium: When did the property become empty?
Are any exceptions likely to apply (e.g. probate, planning restriction)?
disabled-band-reduction: What adaptations does the property have?
Is the adaptation used regularly by the disabled resident?
enforcement: Have you received a Reminder Notice, Final Notice or Liability Order?
Are you currently in financial difficulty or receiving benefits?
